title: ED asthma exacerbation documentation

red_flags.text: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports recording measured vital data, general appearance and time-critical symptoms only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

red_flags.text: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
red_flags.rationale: The exact review and referral section supports recording escalation, specialist discussion or follow-up only when already decided or arranged by the clinician.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

red_flags.text: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.
red_flags.rationale: Virtual-care scope and applicability were reviewed; no automatic in-person examination or emergency treatment was inferred.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

examination.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.
examination.rationale: The exact DHA asthma symptoms section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed risk context for a suspected exacerbation without inferring severity class or diagnosis.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

investigations.text: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigations section supports documenting existing tests reviewed by the clinician without recommending a test.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38

source_limitations: Prior diagnosis and baseline control, trigger, medication use and response, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, peak flow, auscultation, severity classification, treatment, dose, reassessment, observation and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
